titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Issues of Peace and War Pamphlet Collection
Issues of Peace and War Pamphlet Collection
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Martha Lund Smalley and Jerrald Townsend

controlnote.p: This encoded findingaid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday May 16, 2021 at 22:00
Finding aid revised to replace outdated or harmful descriptive language. See the processing note for more information.

did.unittitle: Issues of Peace and War Pamphlet Collection
did.abstract: The collection includes material from more than 170 organizations, as well as topically arranged materials. This open collection of primarily printed material centers on a core of pamphlets, reprints and notes donated to the Divinity Library by Professors Roland H. Bainton and Kenneth Scott Latourette and by Yale alumni Dana Dawson (MA 1942), Ernest Lefever (BD 1945) and Vernon H. Holloway (BD 1936, PhD 1949).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Issues of Peace and War Pamphlet Collection (RG 61), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This open collection of primarily printed material centers on a core of pamphlets, reprints and notes donated to the Divinity Library by Professors Roland H. Bainton and Kenneth Scott Latourette and by Yale alumni Dana Dawson (MA 1942), Ernest Lefever (BD 1945) and Vernon H. Holloway (BD 1936, PhD 1949). Much of the material was apparently gathered by Lefever and Holloway in connection with their academic work. While the majority of the material is related to the issues of the Second World War, documents from as early as 1818 and as late as 1968 are also included. The collection is particularly valuable for its focus on the contributions of American religious organizations and church leaders to the discussion and action surrounding such issues as conscientious objection, civilian public service camps, military training, disarmament, and reconstruction.

The first series includes material from more than 170 organizations and is arranged alphabetically by the name of the organization. Series II contains topically arranged material which is not directly related to any particular organization. The topics of Series II include conscription, disarmament, international relations, Japanese American incarceration, and reconstruction, as well as a large "general" category which is subdivided by format.

The major American Protestant denominations are represented among the organizations of Series I, as well as prominent religious and secular peace organizations and various more eccentric groups. Most fully documented are the American Friends Service Committee and the Fellowship of Reconciliation. The views and activities of individuals such as Roland H. Bainton, John C. Bennett, John Foster Dulles, G. Sherwood Eddy, Harry Emerson Fosdick, John Haynes Holmes, Kenneth Scott Latourette, A.J. Muste, Reinhold Niebuhr, Kirby Page, Norman Thomas, Paul Tillich, and D. Elton Trueblood are documented in the collection.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This finding aid was revised in 2021 to address outdated or harmful descriptive language relating to Japanese American incarceration during World War II. Previous versions of this finding aid may be available. Please contact the Yale University Divinity School Library for details. If you have questions or comments about these revisions, please contact the Yale University Divinity School Library or the Archival and Manuscript Description Committee. For more information on reparative archival description at Yale, see Yale's Statement on Harmful Language in Archival Description

did.unittitle: Algerian Front of National Liberation Delegation
American Committee for Christian Refugees, Inc.
American League Against War and Fascism
Including National Catholic Welfare Conference - War Relief Services
Center for the Study of Democratic Institutions
Central Board for Conscientious Objectors (Great Britain)
Central Bureau for European Interchurch A.D.
Central Bureau for Relief of Evangelical Churches of Europe (Hilfswerk)
Christian Conference on War and Peace
Civilian Public Service (see also: C.P.S. Camps, Friends, A.F.S.C. C.P.S., Mennonite C.P.S.)
Churchmen's Committee for a Christian Peace
Citizen's Committee for Universal Military Training
Citizen's League for America and the Allies
Clergy and Laymen Concerned About Vietnam
Commission on Aliens and Prisoners of War
Commission to Study the Organization of Peace
Committee on American Principles and Fair Play
Committee on National Security and Fair Play
Committee on the War and the Religious Outlook
Committee to Oppose the Austin-Wadsworth Bill
Committee to Oppose Conscription of Women
Conference on the Church and War
Conference of Theologians on Atomic Energy
Congregational Christian Churches - Council for Social Action
Council of Christian Pacifist Groups (Great Britain)
Evangelical and Reformed Church Commission on Christian Social Action
Federal Council of the Churches of Christ in the Americas (FCCCA)
Commission on Church and Social Services
Commission on International Justice and Goodwill
Commission to Study the Basis of a Just and Durable Peace - Miscellaneous

Department of International Justice and Goodwill
Fellowship for a Christian Social Order
Fraternal Council of Negro Churches in America
International Consultative Group for Peace and Disarmament
International Peace Seminar in the Raday College of Budapest
Jewish organizations, including Jewish Peace Fellowship
Lawyers' Committee to Keep U.S. Out of War
Layman's Movement for a Christian World
Methodist Church Commission on World Peace
Michigan Labor Committee Against Peace-time Conscription
Mouvement International de la Reconciliation Branche Française
National Committee for a Free Europe, Inc.
National Committee on Food for the Small Democracies
National Committee on the Churches and World Peace
National Conference on the Churches and World Peace
National Council for the Prevention of War - miscellaneous
National Council for the Prevention of War - "Peace Action"
National Council for Reduction of Armaments
National Council of Congregational Churches, Commission on International Relations
National Japanese American Student Relocation Council
National Service Board for Religious Objectors - miscellaneous
National Service Board for Religious Objectors - "Reporter"
Non-Partisan Council to Win the Peace
Research Exchange in the Prevention of War
Special Senate Committee on Atomic Energy
General Committee on Army and Navy
Women's International League for Peace and Freedom
World Alliance for International Friendship through the Church
World Conference for International Peace through Religion (formerly Universal Religious Peace Conference)

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The United States declared war on Japan in 1941 following an attack by Japan on the United States naval base at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii. Under authority granted by Executive Order 9066, issued in February 1942, the federal government incarcerated over 120,000 people of Japanese descent in American concentration camps.
